steps.title: Spot the predictable explanation
steps.content: The passage states Rogerson's calculation was more accurate, then explains why—the 1920 method used rudimentary equipment. The clue is that this explanation is expected: naturally, primitive tools would yield less accurate results.

steps.title: Apply the clue to the choices
steps.content: ”Of course” acknowledges that the explanation is expected, fitting the natural relationship between rudimentary equipment and lower accuracy. The others denote purpose, similarity, or finality. That matches choice B — B is correct.
